race.name: Granfondo Campagnolo Roma
race.tagline: Start at the Colosseum. Climb the Castelli Romani. Finish at Caracalla. Campagnolo's Roman holiday on two wheels.

vitals.registration: Online via granfondoroma.com. Early registration ~EUR55; late registration ~EUR75. Limited to 6,000-7,500 riders depending on edition.
vitals.feed_zones: Feed zones at strategic points through the Castelli Romani — water, fruit, energy bars, Italian pastries. The Caracalla Village Expo runs for three days around the event with food, bike culture, and sponsors.
vitals.cutoff_time: Generous cutoff with broom wagon support on course.
route_options: Gran Fondo: 123 km (76 mi), ~2,000 m gain — 4 timed climbs through the Castelli Romani
Medio Fondo: 62 km (39 mi) — 'In Bici ai Castelli Romani' — shorter loop through the Roman hills

climate.primary: Early October in Rome — warm, dry, ideal cycling weather
climate.description: Early October in Rome is cycling weather at its finest. Average highs of 22C (72F), lows around 13C (55F), with 6-7 hours of daily sunshine. The Romans call these late-warm days 'ottobrate' — a gift of almost-summer conditions well into autumn. Humidity averages 60-70%, comfortable by Mediterranean standards. Rainfall is possible (88mm average for October, across ~7 days), but the first half of the month is statistically drier. The 7:15 AM start is cool but not cold. By the time you hit the Castelli Romani climbs mid-morning, conditions are warm and pleasant.
challenges: Occasional October rain — 88mm average spread across 7 days in the month
Morning cool at 7:15 AM start (13-15C) — arm warmers for the first hour
Afternoon warmth if the ride extends past noon (22-25C)
Wind exposure on the open roads south of Rome

terrain.primary: Paved Italian roads through Rome and the Castelli Romani volcanic hills
terrain.surface: The opening and closing kilometers through central Rome are a mix of excellent tarmac and occasional cobblestone sections near ancient monuments. The Castelli Romani roads are standard Italian provincial quality — well-maintained tarmac with occasional rough patches on narrower hill roads. The climbs are short and steep rather than long and sustained. All paved throughout.
features: Panoramica (Lake Albano) — 2.3 km at 5.9% average, 8% max. The opening climb with views across the volcanic crater lake
Murus (Rocca di Papa) — 1.1 km at 10.3% average, 16% max. The signature wall — short, savage, and relentless
Rocca Priora (Trofeo Corriere dello Sport) — 6 km at 6.3% average, 13% max. The longest and most sustained climb
Rostrum (Montecompatri) — 0.72 km at 12% average, 18% max. A brutal wall that comes when your legs are already gone
Flat timed section along Pratoni del Vivaro — 8 km flat sprint section
Via dei Fori Imperiali start — riding past the Colosseum and Roman Forum at dawn

fondo_rating.scoring_notes: Migrated from gravel-race-automation. Original score: 59. adventure dimension dropped, road_surface added. Score recalculated: 59.

course_description.character: A tale of two halves: the theatrical departure through ancient Rome, then the athletic challenge of the Castelli Romani volcanic hills. The opening kilometers are pure spectacle — riding past the Colosseum, through the Roman Forum, and out of the city on closed roads. The route then turns southeast toward the Alban Hills, a cluster of volcanic ridges, crater lakes, and medieval hilltop towns that have served as Rome's countryside retreat since antiquity. Four timed climbs provide the competitive structure: the Panoramica along Lake Albano, the savage Murus at Rocca di Papa, the sustained Rocca Priora ascent, and the vicious Rostrum wall at Montecompatri. Between climbs, the roads roll through vineyards, chestnut forests, and small towns. The return to Rome delivers you to the Terme di Caracalla — the ruins of Emperor Caracalla's 3rd-century bath complex.
course_description.signature_challenge: The Murus at Rocca di Papa — 1.1 km at 10.3% average with 16% maximum. It is the climb that defines the Granfondo Campagnolo Roma, the one that separates riders who came for the Colosseum photos from those who came to race. But the real test is the cumulative effect: Panoramica to soften you, Murus to break you, Rocca Priora to grind you down, Rostrum to finish you off. Four climbs in 25 km, none longer than 6 km, all of them steep.
suffering_zones: Rocca Priora — Trofeo Corriere dello Sport

logistics.airport: Rome Fiumicino (FCO) — 30 minutes to city center (major international hub, direct flights from everywhere). Rome Ciampino (CIA) — 25 minutes (budget airlines, Ryanair hub).
logistics.lodging_strategy: Stay in Rome's historic center within walking distance of the Fori Imperiali start. The Colosseum/Monti area and Trastevere are excellent bases — hotels range from EUR150-300/night in October. For budget options, look at Testaccio or San Giovanni neighborhoods (EUR80-150/night, 15-20 minutes by metro). Book 3-6 months ahead for October — it is peak shoulder season in Rome.
logistics.food: Rome needs no food advice. Cacio e pepe, carbonara, supplì, pizza al taglio, espresso at every corner. For carb loading: Roscioli, Da Enzo in Trastevere, Tonnarello. The Castelli Romani produce Frascati wine and porchetta — the post-ride meal at a Castelli trattoria is mandatory. The Caracalla expo village has food vendors for the event weekend.

history.origin_story: The Granfondo Campagnolo Roma was born in 2012, when Campagnolo — the Vicenza-based component maker that has equipped Tour de France, Giro d'Italia, and World Championship winners since 1933 — partnered to create a gran fondo worthy of the Eternal City. The inaugural edition attracted 5,500 riders from 20 countries, proving that Rome's ancient streets could host a modern cycling mass-start event. The concept was ambitious: close the roads around the Colosseum and the Roman Forum, then send thousands of cyclists into the volcanic hills of the Castelli Romani before returning to the Terme di Caracalla. The event grew quickly — a 7,500-rider cap was introduced for the 2013 edition with 2,000 slots reserved for international participants. The brand extended to Granfondo Campagnolo events in San Diego and New York. The 2019 edition suffered a devastating blow when it was cancelled just 24 hours before the start due to failure to secure municipal route agreements — a logistical failure that damaged the event's reputation. The COVID pandemic further disrupted continuity. The event's future editions remain subject to the complex bureaucracy of closing roads in a capital city.
notable_moments: 2012: Inaugural edition — 5,500 riders from 20 countries ride through Rome for the first time
2013: Field cap raised to 7,500 with 2,000 international slots — demand outstrips supply
Caracalla Village Expo grows to 40,000+ attendees — becomes a major Roman cycling festival
Brand extends to Granfondo Campagnolo events in San Diego and New York
Damiano Cunego (Giro d'Italia champion) serves as event ambassador and route consultant
2019: Edition cancelled 24 hours before the start — municipal permits not secured
COVID pandemic (2020-2021) disrupts continuity alongside many global cycling events
history.reputation: A glamorous gran fondo in an extraordinary setting, backed by cycling's most storied component maker. The reputation is built on Rome's iconic start, not on organizational reliability. The 2019 cancellation remains a scar.

biased_opinion.summary: The Granfondo Campagnolo Roma is not a great race. It is a great experience wrapped in a decent race. You start at the Colosseum at dawn. You ride through the Roman Forum on closed roads. You climb into the Castelli Romani — the volcanic hills where ancient Romans retreated from the summer heat, where Frascati wine comes from, where Lake Albano fills a crater that last erupted 36,000 years ago. You ride walls as steep as 18%. You finish at the ruins of the Terme di Caracalla. Campagnolo puts its name on it, which means Italian cycling heritage is baked into the experience. The cycling itself? It is 123 km with 2,000m of climbing — honest but unremarkable by Italian gran fondo standards. The setting is what makes it.
strengths: Starting at the Colosseum and riding through the Roman Forum at dawn — genuinely once-in-a-lifetime
Campagnolo sponsorship brings legitimate Italian cycling heritage and production quality
The Castelli Romani climbs are short, steep, and beautiful — volcanic hills with crater lakes and vineyards
October in Rome is near-perfect cycling weather — the 'ottobrate' at their finest
Rome is Rome — extend the trip and the cycling is just one layer of a world-class destination
weaknesses: The 2019 edition was cancelled 24 hours before the start due to permit failures — a serious organizational black mark
Post-pandemic continuity has been uncertain — the event's long-term future is not guaranteed
Rome is expensive: accommodation, food, and transport add up quickly
Field depth is shallow — this is a tourist sportive, not a competitive racing event
123 km with 2,000m climbing is honest but unexceptional — northern Italian GFs are significantly harder
biased_opinion.bottom_line: Come for Rome, stay for the Colosseum start, enjoy the Castelli Romani hills, appreciate the Campagnolo heritage. Do not come expecting a world-class racing event or Alpine-scale suffering. The Granfondo Campagnolo Roma is the best way to ride a bicycle through one of the world's great cities. That is enough.

final_verdict.one_liner: Start at the Colosseum. Climb the Castelli Romani. Finish at Caracalla. Campagnolo's Roman holiday on two wheels.
final_verdict.should_you_race: Yes if riding through Rome on closed roads — past the Colosseum, through the Forum, finishing at Caracalla — is on your cycling bucket list. Yes if you want a well-produced autumn gran fondo in one of the world's great cities. Skip if you want serious climbing or competitive depth — Nove Colli, Maratona, or the Sportful Dolomiti Race are better choices. Skip if the organizers have not confirmed the current edition is actually running.
final_verdict.alternatives: If you want Italian cycling heritage with harder climbing: Gran Fondo Nove Colli (200 km, 9 hills, since 1971). If you want a Dolomites challenge: Maratona dles Dolomites (7 passes, 4,190m climbing). If you want another Italian brand gran fondo: Granfondo Pinarello in Treviso.

youtube_data.search_query: Granfondo Campagnolo Roma gran fondo cycling Rome

videos.title: GRANFONDO CAMPAGNOLO ROMA - OFFICIAL VIDEO
videos.description:
The Granfondo Campagnolo Roma is an event for everyone: amateurs, trained cyclists, enthusiasts of all ages, novices, fans, those who want to learn to ride, families who dream of taking on the best climbs, those who have never cycled and would like to give it a try.
The Granfondo Campagnolo Roma is not merely a cycling race. It is an event, where the competition is only one element of a weekend dedicated to cycling culture in all its facets. A competitive event, albeit amateur, joins a fun cycle, a vintage hike and other small and large events organized within a real “bicycle village”.
The race is preceded by two days of events at the Campagnolo Roma village, set against the backdrop of the Baths of Caracalla. The main event, the Granfondo, is open to all current members of the Italian Cycling Federation (FCI) or sports promotion bodies. All riders must possess a medical certificate for the practice of competitive cycling. Foreign cyclists must present a club membership card that is recognized by the UCI.
Professional and Elite athletes are allowed to participate for promotional purposes only and at the invitation of the organizers. Former Elite athletes can participate, but with very specific constraints, specified in the regulations. “In Bici ai Castelli”, however, is open to everybody, including non-members of the Federation. A valid medical certificate attesting to the good health of the rider is still required. Any type of bicycle can be used to participate in this event.
In the Granfondo and “In Bici ai Castelli” e-bikes are permitted: cyclists riding e-bikes are admitted as cycle-tourists. Each cyclist is required to comply with the rules of the road along the mandatory route.
The Granfondo Campagnolo takes place in Rome and surroundings along a 120 km route, with 1700m elevation gain: cyclists will ride through history, through the magnificent scenery of the Colosseum and the Roman Forum. The peloton then passes Piazza Venezia, the Vittorio Emanuele Monument, the Capitol, Circus Maximus and Appia Antica, which leads out of town. At that point cyclists will discover the beautiful landscape of the Castelli Romani and Lake Albano which reflects the summer residence of the Pope in its waters, before returning to Rome and the Baths of Caracalla.
The “In Bici ai Castelli” event is an easy route to Lake Albano by bike: it’s a fun cycle of 62 km.
Rome, a great capital, rich in history and monuments, has its own cycling festival. Granfondo Campagnolo and its events will be a day out, meeting lots of people, getting to know the bike world, discovering and appreciating Rome and its surroundings, demonstrating that cycling is an extraordinary way to promote quality of life in this big city.
One of the most important new elements of this edition of the Granfondo Campagnolo Roma is the idea of the “Imperiale, the Appian Way”, for all heroic cycling fans. The cycle is reserved for pre-1987 bikes and cyclists wearing vintage gear. It will cover the same route as In Bici ai Castelli, from Rome to Albano and back, but with an exciting difference: the last part runs along the Ancient Appian Way, on the same route that was once used by the Giro del Lazio professional race.

Rome Gran Fondo - shot on #gopro

Check minutes 3, 9, 10, 12 for some action
videos.curation_reason: Full first-person GoPro race footage providing authentic ride experience and course preview.

videos.title: Granfondo Campagnolo Roma | 2019
videos.description:
Granfondo Campagnolo Roma 2019,  October 11-13.
Discover the Campagnolo stand in Rome: http://bit.ly/32aY8sY

All roads lead to Rome. Don’t miss your chance to ride with us this Sunday the @granfondocampagnoloroma. We are waiting for you just in front the Coloseum 😊

Campagnolo awaits you at the stand which functions as centrepiece of the event, immersed in the fantastic setting of the Circo Massimo. The Campagnolo staff will present products from the new 12 Speed range, in both mechanical and electronic versions as well as the new Campagnolo WTO wheels. You will also have the opportunity to buy selected collector's items, normally exclusively available for sale on our e-commerce site. There will also be a chance to get together with the enthusiasts registered in the Campagnolo Riders Club.

videos.title: ROMA - Gran fondo campagnolo Roma - video ufficiale
videos.description:
Rome is now!!
It's the dream of every granfondo rider: a course completely closed to traffic.
But the dream could become reality on October 13, 2013.
The meetings between the organizing committee of the Granfondo Campagnolo Roma and the municipalities affected by the event are moving forward at a rapid pace.
videos.curation_reason: Official video with good view count that highlights the unique closed-road format through Rome.
